mission.title: keda: Emit Kuberentes Events on major KEDA events
mission.description: Emit Kuberentes Events on major KEDA events. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current keda deployment
steps.description:
Verify your keda version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
helm list -n keda 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working keda installation.

steps.title: Review keda configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keda configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get configmap -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keda
```
KEDA controller should emit Kuberentes Events on major events.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Emit Kuberentes Events on major KEDA events
steps.description:
This PR adds the following events:

For both `ScaledObjects` and `ScaledJobs`:

- `Ready`
- `CheckFailed`
- `Deleted`
- `ScalersStarted`
- `ScalersRestarted`
- `ScalersStopped`

For `ScaledObjects`:

- `ScaleTargetActivated`
- `ScaleTargetDeactivated`
- `ScaleTargetActivationFailed`
- `ScaleTargetDeactivationFailed`

For `ScaledJobs`:

- `JobsCreated`

If this list looks okay, I'll open a docs PR to list them in there.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keda -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keda
kubectl get events -n keda --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Emit Kuberentes Events on major KEDA events" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This PR adds the following events:

For both `ScaledObjects` and `ScaledJobs`:

- `Ready`
- `CheckFailed`
- `Deleted`
- `ScalersStarted`
- `ScalersRestarted`
- `ScalersStopped`

For `ScaledObjects`:

- `ScaleTargetActivated`
- `ScaleTargetDeactivated`
- `ScaleTargetActivationFailed`
- `ScaleTargetDeactivationFailed`

For `ScaledJobs`:

- `JobsCreated`

If this list looks okay, I'll open a docs PR

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keda installed or the issue environment reproducible.
